name: Elder Mother
description: The Elder Mother, or Elder tree (Sambucus nigra), holds a prominent place in European folklore and tradition, deeply intertwined with magic, healing, and protection. Considered a sacred tree, it was often seen as inhabited by a spirit, the Elder Mother herself, who could bring both blessings and curses. Respect was paramount; one would always ask permission before cutting its branches or using its wood. The tree was believed to protect against evil spirits and misfortune, often planted near homes for this purpose. Its flowers and berries were used extensively in folk medicine for their healing properties, treating colds, flu, and skin ailments. The Elder Mother was revered as a guardian spirit, and rituals surrounding the tree were common, involving offerings and prayers for good health and protection. Its connection to death and rebirth also made it significant in funerary rites. The Elder tree represents the cyclical nature of life and the power of the natural world, embodying both the nurturing and protective aspects of the feminine divine. Its continued presence in folklore highlights its enduring importance in European cultural heritage. The act of felling an elder tree without proper respect was believed to bring bad luck or even death to the perpetrator and their family. Even today, some people acknowledge the spirit of the tree before harvesting its fruits or flowers. The tree’s shadow was also thought to possess protective qualities.
subtitle: Sacred tree of healing and protection

keyMyths.title: The Elder Mother's Wrath
keyMyths.description: Disrespecting the Elder tree by cutting it down without permission or proper offerings could bring misfortune or illness to the offender and their family, as the Elder Mother would seek retribution. This served as a deterrent to unnecessary destruction of the tree.

sources.author: Jacob Grimm
sources.description: A comprehensive exploration of Germanic mythology, including references to the Elder Mother and her associated beliefs.
sources.text: A comprehensive exploration of Germanic mythology, including references to the Elder Mother and her associated beliefs.

sources.author: Richard Folkard
